data.title: Key facts: France suspends pension reform; BNP Paribas names new Nordic head
data.description:
• France's suspension of pension reforms has raised concerns in banking, affecting BNP Paribas due to expected higher bond spreads and increased funding costs.1
  • BNP Paribas appointed Peter Wikström as Head of Nordic Investment Banking, starting January 2026, to enhance its Nordic presence. He has over 20 years of investment banking experience.2

data.title: Key facts: Euronext CEO backs European stock exchange; retracts grain data
data.description:
• Euronext CEO Stéphane Boujnah endorsed a proposal for a European stock exchange, emphasizing Euronext's dedication to consolidating markets in Europe to enhance liquidity for businesses.1
  • Euronext has retracted its recent weekly data on grain derivatives due to a technical glitch that caused a rise in long positions, promising an updated report after corrections.2

data.title: Key facts: EVOQ and Sanofi sign $500M deal; Owkin launches AI tool K Pro
data.description:
• EVOQ Therapeutics and Sanofi have signed a deal worth over $500 million in milestones and royalties, with Sanofi managing global development and commercialization of the products.1
  • Owkin has launched K Pro, an AI tool for biopharma, validated with top firms like Sanofi, to improve decision-making in drug development and biomedical research.2

data.title: Key facts: Deutsche Bank to sell $1.2B debt; upgrades Telecom Italia to buy
data.description:
• Deutsche Bank, in collaboration with Goldman Sachs, is preparing to sell $1.2 billion in debt associated with the Finastra unit.1
  • Deutsche Bank upgraded Telecom Italia from 'hold' to 'buy', raising the price target from 0.34 to 0.62 euros, highlighting strong upside potential amid expected telecom consolidation.2

data.title: Key facts: UBS Group targets premium consumption; opens Middle East office; names AI chief
data.description:
• UBS Group is focused on premium consumption in autos and goods, sees potential in consumption tech, and finds the defense sector attractive, especially for mid-cap IT services.1
  • UBS Group has launched a new office in the Middle East, focusing on ultra-high-net-worth clients and corporations with advanced financial solutions and advisory services.2
  • UBS Group has named Daniele Magazzeni from JPMorgan Chase as chief AI officer, starting in 2026, to lead its AI strategy for boosting employee productivity and client experience.3

data.title: Key facts: Novartis' Fabhalta gains accelerated approval; faces new tariffs
data.description:
• Novartis' drug Fabhalta met primary goals in a Phase III trial, significantly slowing IgA nephropathy progression. It received U.S. accelerated approval for 2024 and aims for full approval in 2026.12
  • Novartis may be affected by new U.S. tariffs on Swiss imports, set at 39%, which could impact drugmakers amid efforts to reduce the U.S. trade deficit.3

data.title: Key facts: Iberdrola sells 49% stake in UK wind farm; Cox buys Mexican assets
data.description:
• Iberdrola is selling a 49% stake in its East Anglia Two offshore wind farm in the UK, valued at about 5 billion euros. Bank of America and BBVA are advising.1
  • Cox has purchased Iberdrola's Mexican assets for $4.2 billion, part of a $6.4 billion investment in water and renewable energy, targeting the Mexican market through 2028.2

data.title: Key facts: Deutsche Bank upgrades Telecom Italia; merger with Iliad could boost shares
data.description:
• Deutsche Bank upgraded Telecom Italia (TIM) from 'hold' to 'buy' and raised the target price for its shares and savings shares to 0.62 euros, up from 0.34 euros.1
  • Deutsche Bank estimates that merging Telecom Italia with Iliad Italy could offer over 50% upside, while remaining independent may still yield a 30% gain.2

data.title: Key facts: Airbus acquires Spirit facilities; Germany boosts defense; Thales satellite deal unfinalized
data.description:
• Airbus has gained approval from French regulators for its acquisition of Spirit AeroSystems facilities, enhancing production control and supply chains ahead of finalizing the April deal.1
  • Germany is set to enhance its defense capabilities through the acquisition of 20 new Eurofighters, with Airbus participating in the consortium alongside BAE Systems and Leonardo.2
  • Thales has not completed a satellite deal with Airbus, despite reports of an upcoming agreement, as the partnership aims to form a European satellite alliance to rival Starlink.3

Diesel brand owner OTB is positive on China and in no rush to IPO
data.description:
By Casey Hall

OTB Group, the parent of Italian denim specialist Diesel and owner of luxury brands including Maison Margiela, is positive on the Chinese market and is investing more in it, founder and chairman Renzo Rosso said.

Rosso, in China to mark Diesel's 20 years in that market, said he is continuing to invest in China in a down market, rejigging OTB's retail footprint by closing some stores, but continuing to open in new and better locations.

"I am positive. I think if the market is going like this, it can be an opportunity because we can have a better space available for a better price that wasn't available before," he said. "My vision right now is to invest in the country. I believe in the country; it is so big, so important."

Rosso was speaking to Reuters on Thursday ahead of a dinner for around 100 people with a view of Shanghai's famous skyline.

"This year, we are doing well compared with the market. Everybody is minus, we are a little plus, so we are quite happy," he added.

China's consumer appetite has been in focus for global brands, especially in the high-end and luxury sectors which count on Chinese nationals for around one-third of global purchases.

In recent years, consumer demand in the world's second-largest economy has been dampened by a prolonged property market crisis and concerns about job security and wage growth in an economy buffeted by factors including slowing growth and a trade war with the U.S.

In an indication of how vital China is to luxury, reports from industry bellwether LVMH earlier this week that Chinese demand is improving sparked an $80 billion stock market rally for the sector.

OTB, or Only the Brave, is also home to luxury brand Jil Sander, and as a group reported turnover of 1.8 billion euros ($2.11 billion) in 2024, a fall of 4.4%.

In 2024, OTB postponed plans for a public listing originally slated for this year, saying it would probably delay it until 2026. Rosso remains in no hurry to go public.

"First of all, I don't need the money, I am cash positive," he said. "There's no rush, it can be one year, two years, I don't care. If I feel good, then yes."

($1 = 0.8551 euros)

data.title: Dollar Heads for Weekly Loss
data.description:
The dollar index fell to around 98.2 on Friday and was on track for its biggest weekly decline since July, pressured by intensifying US-China trade tensions, the prolonged US government shutdown, and bets on Federal Reserve interest rate cuts.

China accused the US of deliberately stoking panic over rare earth export controls but signaled willingness to resume trade talks.

Meanwhile, the government shutdown entered its third week, delaying key economic data that could guide policy decisions.

Fed Governor Christopher Waller said Thursday he supports another rate cut this month amid mounting labor market risks, while Fed Governor Stephen Miran called for more aggressive easing.

The Fed’s Beige Book also highlighted emerging economic strains, including rising layoffs and weaker spending among middle- and lower-income households.

data.title: Bangkok Commercial Asset Management Target Price Lowered to THB7.50 From THB8.40 by Thanachart Securities >BAM.TH
Bangkok Commercial Asset Management Cut to Hold From Buy by Thanachart Securities >BAM.TH
99 Speed Mart to Gain From Rising Cash Aid, Expansion Plans — Market Talk
data.description: 99 Speed Mart Retail stands to benefit most from Malaysia's growing government cash-aid program, which could drive earnings growth and support further valuation gains, RHB IB analyst Soong Wei Siang says in a note. Outlet expansion into underserved areas is expected to sustain earnings growth of 15% over the next three years, he says. As one of the major distributors of Nestle products, the retailer should also see stronger sales as sentiment toward the company's products normalizes. With its wide store network and appeal among price-sensitive consumers, 99 Speed Mart is well-positioned to capture demand from lower-income groups and downtrading trends amid high inflation. RHB raises 99 Speed Mart's target price to MYR3.56 from MYR2.81, while maintaining a buy rating on the stock. Shares are 1.9% higher at MYR3.19. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

Starbucks Evaluating Offers for China Business
data.description:
By Megan Cheah

Starbucks is assessing bids from five parties for its China business as it seeks a partner to navigate the country's competitive coffee landscape.

"We've had very strong interest from multiple, high-quality partners all of whom share our confidence in the long-term growth potential of Starbucks in China," said a Starbucks spokesperson.

The spokesperson said that five bidders had provided bids, and the company was in the process of evaluating them.

The company estimates the value of the deal to be over $10 billion, including royalties, the upfront investment by a potential partner, and Starbucks's retained stake in the China business.

The news was first reported by the Financial Times.

The Seattle-based beverages giant is facing intense competition in China, one of its main markets, with local chains such as Luckin Coffee opening more stores, offering cheaper drinks and easy-to-use mobile apps.

At the same time, more U.S.-based companies have been seeking to exit or pare their exposure to China as trade tensions rise between the world's two largest economies.

Write to Megan Cheah at megan.cheah@wsj.com

data.title: Parkway Corporate Secures Site for Queensland Brine Management Complex
data.description: Parkway Corporate  has secured a 10-hectare site for its planned Queensland Brine Management Complex (QBMC), a landmark project to transform coal seam gas waste brine into valuable green chemicals. The site, which Parkway obtained through a binding option-to-lease agreement, will host the company’s integrated brine processing and electrochemical facilities, providing a long-term solution to one of the energy industry’s most persistent environmental challenges. Chief executive officer Mr Bahay Ozcakmak said the acquisition marks a critical milestone toward establishing Parkway as a developer of strategic brine infrastructure. Strategic Location in Heart of Gas Industry The site lies within Queensland’s Western Downs region at the centre of the state’s coal seam gas (CSG) industry and adjacent to major infrastructure including high-voltage power lines, substations, and a planned renewable energy precinct. It also sits near key regional roads and the Kenya Chinchilla water pipeline, offering options for reusing treated water in agriculture. The location allows for efficient integration of the QBMC’s two main components — the brine management and electrochemical processing units — into one highly synergistic industrial complex. Parkway subsidiary Queensland Brine Solutions will develop the QBMC to process up to half of all brine produced by Queensland’s CSG industry, including legacy waste currently stored in evaporation ponds, using Parkway’s proprietary processes to convert waste brine into commercial products such as industrial salts and green chemicals. The facility will also have potential to expand its capacity to treat waste brine from emerging gas projects in the Taroom Trough basin and other regions. Progress on Approvals and Partnerships Development approvals and environmental assessments are in motion, with pre-lodgement meetings yielding positive feedback from regulators. Parkway believes the QBMC could become Australia’s first purpose-built facility offering permanent, compliant liquid waste disposal for the gas industry once approved, and is also advancing partnership discussions with upstream CSG producers, downstream chemical manufacturers, and infrastructure providers. Mr Ozcakmak said securing the site represents the culmination of more than two years of planning and site evaluation. “Parkway is now another step closer to becoming a developer of highly strategic brine processing infrastructure that underpins the long-term sustainability of Queensland’s $100 billion gas industry,” he said. Parkway expects project execution could begin as early as mid-2026, subject to approvals and funding, positioning the QBMC as a cornerstone asset in the company’s transition to large-scale commercial operations.
